description: Operator-level Tier-1 settings that don't belong to any single Vibe app — Tailscale toggle, DNS provider for ACME, update channel, default log level. Per addendum admin-config-surface.md §2.1, these are appliance-wide and live in /opt/vibe/env/appliance.env.

settings.name: TAILSCALE_ENABLED
settings.doc: Whether Tailscale is enabled on the host. Read-only mirror of state.config.tailscale; toggle here triggers the Tailscale up/down postSaveJob (v1.2 special-case flow).

ui.label: Tailscale
ui.helpText: Enables tailnet-only HTTPS access (https://<host>.<tailnet>.ts.net) alongside the primary mode. Toggle ON requires a Tailscale auth key.

settings.name: TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY
settings.doc: Tailscale auth key for unattended `tailscale up`. Required when TAILSCALE_ENABLED transitions false→true. Cleared after successful authentication.

settings.name: DNS_PROVIDER
settings.doc: ACME challenge type. http-01 (default) needs port 80 reachable from the public internet; cloudflare uses DNS-01 wildcard certs and requires the custom Caddy build (caddy/Dockerfile.cloudflare) — see PLAN.md §4.1.

ui.label: DNS / cert challenge
ui.helpText: http-01 is the default and works with the stock caddy:2-alpine image. cloudflare requires the opt-in custom Caddy build with the Cloudflare DNS plugin baked in.

options.label: Cloudflare DNS-01 (wildcard; needs custom Caddy build)

settings.name: CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN
settings.doc: Cloudflare API token with Zone:DNS:Edit. Today writes to /opt/vibe/env/appliance.env via the standard Settings save flow. The Caddy container's env_file list is updated to include appliance.env so the cloudflare plugin reads the value at runtime. (Pre-v1.2 operators using bootstrap.sh --cloudflare-api-token can continue — that path writes to shared.env, which still wins as a legacy override.)

ui.label: Cloudflare API token
ui.helpText: Required when DNS provider is cloudflare. Token must have Zone:DNS:Edit on the appliance's domain.

settings.name: DDNS_PROVIDER
settings.doc: Dynamic-DNS provider for non-static-IP installs. When set, the console runs a background updater that pushes the appliance's current public IP to the chosen provider on a regular cadence so A records stay accurate across ISP IP rotations.

ui.label: Dynamic DNS provider
ui.helpText: Pick 'namecheap' if your domain is registered with Namecheap (BasicDNS, PremiumDNS, or FreeDNS) and the host is on a residential/dynamic IP. Leave 'none' for static-IP installs. Incompatible with Cloudflare DNS-01 (which requires Cloudflare nameservers).

settings.name: NAMECHEAP_DDNS_DOMAIN
settings.doc: Apex domain managed by Namecheap, e.g. example.com. The updater will write A records for the bare domain (@), www, and every enabled app's manifest.subdomain.

ui.label: Namecheap domain
ui.helpText: Apex only — no scheme, no subdomain. The appliance auto-publishes one A record per enabled app (subdomain from each app's manifest), plus @ and www.

settings.name: NAMECHEAP_DDNS_PASSWORD
settings.doc: Per-domain Dynamic-DNS password from Namecheap (NOT your account password). Find at Domain List → Manage → Advanced DNS → Dynamic DNS, then mouse over the password circle to reveal.

ui.label: Namecheap DDNS password
ui.helpText: Auto-generated by Namecheap when DDNS is toggled on for the domain. Distinct from your account password. Sent in the URL of an HTTPS GET to dynamicdns.park-your-domain.com — Namecheap's only published transport.

settings.name: DDNS_INTERVAL_MIN
settings.doc: How often (minutes) the appliance checks the host's public IP and, if it changed, calls the DDNS provider. Range 5–60. The check itself is cheap; the provider call only fires on actual IP change.

ui.label: DDNS check interval (minutes)
ui.helpText: 5–60. Smaller values mean faster recovery from ISP IP rotation but more public-IP probes. Updates only call Namecheap when the IP actually changed, so this value is mostly about how quickly the appliance notices a rotation.

settings.name: TZ
settings.doc: IANA time zone for the appliance host. Cascades to per-app TZ env vars unless an app overrides it (Vibe-Payroll-Time declares TZ as appliance:both).

ui.label: Time zone
ui.helpText: e.g. America/Chicago. Drives log timestamps and per-app TZ defaults.

settings.name: UPDATE_CHANNEL
settings.doc: Which image-tag track update.sh follows when the operator clicks Update. stable = latest released GHCR tag; beta = latest pre-release; pinned = no auto-suggest, operator picks tags manually.

ui.label: Update channel
ui.helpText: Affects what 'Update available' badges look at. Use pinned for production-critical hosts where update timing must be deliberate.

settings.name: LOG_LEVEL_DEFAULT
settings.doc: Default log level cascading to per-app LOG_LEVEL env. Apps with their own LOG_LEVEL declared (Vibe-MyBooks does) override this.

options.label: debug (high volume; use only when investigating)

settings.name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
settings.doc: Anthropic API key for the appliance support assistant ('Ask Claude' on the Logs page) and any Vibe app whose manifest declares ui.appliance: "both" on this key. Stored in /opt/vibe/env/appliance.env, mode 600. Operator brings their own key — the appliance never proxies through a vendor key.

ui.label: Anthropic API key
ui.helpText: Powers the 'Ask Claude' button on the Logs page (admin → Logs). Logs sent to Anthropic may include hostnames, IPs, container names, and configuration details — set this only if you'd be willing to share those with a vendor support team. Get a key at https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys.

settings.name: ANTHROPIC_MODEL
settings.doc: Anthropic model used by the 'Ask Claude' button on the Logs page. Haiku is fastest and cheapest; Sonnet is the balanced default for most analyses; Opus is slowest and most expensive but goes deepest on tangled multi-cause failures. Model IDs are sent verbatim to api.anthropic.com/v1/messages.

ui.label: Claude model
ui.helpText: Pick faster/cheaper or slower/deeper based on what you ask. The dropdown is auto-populated from api.anthropic.com/v1/models when an API key is configured; the three baseline options below are the fallback when the live list isn't reachable.

settings.name: CONSOLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_INFO
settings.doc: Admin password rotation widget. Posts to /api/v1/admin/change-admin-password, which scrypt-hashes the new password into the meta table and updates the in-memory comparator immediately — no console restart needed. The boot-time password from shared.env stays as a recovery backstop until the operator re-runs bootstrap.sh --reset-env or hand-edits shared.env.

ui.label: Admin password
ui.helpText: Rotates the admin password without a console restart. Persists to console.sqlite; takes effect on the next request. To also rotate the recovery password baked into /opt/vibe/env/shared.env, run sudo bootstrap.sh --reset-env from the host.
